LanguageData.HHTools_Cascadia_Civil.description: A democratic settlement in the rain-soaked wastes of Cascadia, where the Pacific fog rarely lifts and the coast has been profoundly changed by the bombs and the years since. Settlers here build governed communities against a wilderness that feels older than civilization and indifferent to the concerns of surface dwellers. Trade, elected councils, and communal labor keep these towns functional — and keep their residents occupied enough not to dwell on what moves at the waterline after dark.
LanguageData.HHTools_Cascadia_Civil.label: Civil Cascadia Settlement
LanguageData.HHTools_Cascadia_Raider.description: A raider gang operating in the dense forests and ruined coastal stretches of Cascadia. The terrain works in their favor — pursuit is difficult, disappearances are easy to explain, and legitimate settlements are spread too thin for anyone to come running quickly. The strange things that live deeper in the wetlands are a fact of the landscape that Cascadian raiders have learned to tolerate, more or less.
LanguageData.HHTools_Cascadia_Raider.label: Cascadia Raider
LanguageData.HHTools_Cascadia_Rough.description: An autocratic holdout in the mist-wrapped wilds of Cascadia, ruled by whoever commands enough fear or loyalty to hold it together. The Pacific Northwest was already an isolating landscape before the bombs; what it became afterward is something that most wasteland travelers describe poorly and revisit rarely. These communities are insular, hard, and deeply suspicious of anyone who arrives out of the fog uninvited.
LanguageData.HHTools_Cascadia_Rough.label: Rough Cascadia Settlement
LanguageData.HHTools_Cascadia_Tribal.description: A tribal community living deep in the Cascadian wilderness, among the flooded ruins and forest-covered remnants of the northwest coast. Their stories are full of things that dwell in the dark water and beneath the roots of old-growth trees that should have died centuries ago. Whether these are genuine encounters or generations of isolation and radiation talking, their traditions treat the wilderness with a reverence that borders on dread.
LanguageData.HHTools_Cascadia_Tribal.label: Cascadia Tribe
